breaking_news_suggestions.headline: Dome author adopts our timestamp critique in writing — but doesn't fix it
breaking_news_suggestions.summary: The dome author added a methodology.json file on 2026-04-17 that explicitly scopes what OpenTimestamps proves vs. does not prove — near-verbatim language from our Timestamp Error tab. He now acknowledges OTS does not prove per-claim prospectivity. However, the underlying data structure is unchanged: status_history.json is still the OTS-anchored surface, predictions are still mixed with observations in the same file, and no new per-claim temporal mechanism exists. Our canary planted in Section 5.16 appears to have been read and partially absorbed.
breaking_news_suggestions.why_newsworthy: First time dome author has directly acknowledged the OTS limitation in his own documentation. Validates our canary methodology and creates a stronger framing for Section 5.16.
